title: Outbound / Inbound Phone Calling and Dialer System
excerpt:
Hi…  wanted to see if someone out there could help me.   We currently have an Inside Sales team using Monday CRM for Leads and PhoneBurner for outbound and inbound dialing / text / emails. 
What’s your setup for Calling?…

posts.author: Cur_10 (Currie Gilbert)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi… wanted to see if someone out there could help me. We currently have an Inside Sales team using Monday CRM for Leads and PhoneBurner for outbound and inbound dialing / text / emails.

What’s your setup for Calling? Exploring Integrated Phone dialer systems that can handle: Outbound dialing, Inbound Calls, Texting, and Emails… but keeping all records in Monday. Any great setups out there ?

posts.author: tanner_elevate (Tanner Nelson | Elevate Operations)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Cur_10

We’ve used Aircall in the past, and it works great! They have an app that works really well, but the sales team can also make calls straight from their computer, which makes it super convenient. Here’s a link to see if it’ll work for you guys: Sign up | Aircall

Like I said, we’ve implemented this for some of our clients in the past, so I’m happy to help if you need

Tanner
Tanner@elevateops.io
Monday.com Consultant
Elevate Business Operations

posts.author: SuhanAshraf (Suhan Ashraf)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Cur_10
Just wanted to share our experience with PowerDialer AI since I saw you asking about calling solutions. We switched to them at our sales team about 6 months ago, and it’s made a huge difference in productivity. The browser-based dialing system is really intuitive and our reps just need Chrome or Firefox to make calls. What I particularly like is their local presence dialing feature and the way it syncs with our CRM.
You can check them out here. If you do end up trying them, happy to share some tips on how we configured things for our team. We had a bit of trial and error figuring out the best settings.

posts.author: Gabriel447 (Gabriel)
posts.cookedHtml:
If you’re exploring an all-in-one setup for outbound/inbound calling, SMS, and activity logging inside Monday, here are a few tools teams commonly compare:

CloudTalk

  • Pros: Strong integration with Monday, supports outbound + inbound + SMS, and pushes interactions into Monday in a structured way (which helps keep boards clean and reporting consistent). Also integrates well with a wide range of other CRMs if your stack grows later.

  • Cons: Slightly more configuration options, so it may take a bit more initial setup than very lightweight dialers.

KrispCall

  • Pros: Simple, easy to get started with, good for teams that mainly need calling and basic SMS synced to Monday.

  • Cons: Fewer automation and workflow controls, so complex tracking or multi-step workflows may require extra workarounds.

PhoneBurner

  • Pros: Very strong for high-volume outbound dialing; great if your team’s focus is pure outbound outreach.

  • Cons: Not as comprehensive for inbound or SMS, and syncing all interaction types into Monday can require additional configuration.

Each tool fits a slightly different use case. So the best option depends on whether your team needs a simple dialer or a more unified communication workflow that keeps all call and SMS activity neatly logged inside Monday.

posts.author: kingastersk (kingastersk)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi!
One option is using a custom dialer integrated with Monday CRM. It can manage outbound and inbound calls while keeping lead data in Monday, with call activities synced back to the CRM.

posts.author: edarling85 (Wild 6 | Eric Darling)
posts.cookedHtml: Aircall is awesome! ever since Monday added a few more integration automations that add the calls and texts into the timelines.
